comment_text.sn35.133:1.2: It is unusual, if not unique, to see a female brahmin with a student. Below, she is referred to as the “tutor’s wife” (ācariyabhariyā); compare Chāndogya Upaniṣad 4.10, where a tutor’s wife (ācāryajāyā) discusses the education of the pupil with her husband. Verahaccāni is not an attested clan name, and Sanskrit vairahatya means “manslaughter”. Given that her husband is rather mysteriously absent, perhaps verahaccāni is a term for brahmin ladies whose husbands were murdered. Compare nirvīrā, a woman whose husband and children are dead. If so, it would seem that she did not merely survive her husband’s passing, but stepped into his role as teacher.
comment_text.sn35.133:2.7: She adopts these as signifiers of status. Perhaps as a woman she felt the need to assert her status. Nonetheless, the rules of training forbid monastics from teaching someone wearing slippers (Bu Sk 61), sitting on a high seat (Bu Sk 69), or with head covered (Bu Sk 67).

root_text.sn35.133:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ āyasmā udāyī kāmaṇḍāyaṁ viharati todeyyassa brāhmaṇassa ambavane.
root_text.sn35.133:1.2: Atha kho verahaccānigottāya brāhmaṇiyā antevāsī māṇavako yenāyasmā udāyī tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmatā udāyinā saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.sn35.133:1.3: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi.
root_text.sn35.133:1.4: Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho taṁ māṇavakaṁ āyasmā udāyī dhammiyā kathāya sandassesi samādapesi samuttejesi sampahaṁsesi.
root_text.sn35.133:1.5: Atha kho so māṇavako āyasmatā udāyinā dhammiyā kathāya sandassito samādapito samuttejito sampahaṁsito uṭṭhāyāsanā yena verahaccānigottā brāhmaṇī tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā verahaccānigottaṁ brāhmaṇiṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn35.133:1.7: Samaṇo udāyī dhammaṁ deseti ādikalyāṇaṁ majjhekalyāṇaṁ pariyosānakalyāṇaṁ, sātthaṁ sabyañjanaṁ kevalaparipuṇṇaṁ parisuddhaṁ brahmacariyaṁ pakāsetī”ti.
root_text.sn35.133:2.1: “Tena hi tvaṁ, māṇavaka, mama vacanena samaṇaṁ udāyiṁ nimantehi svātanāya bhattenā”ti.
root_text.sn35.133:2.2: “Evaṁ, bhotī”ti kho so māṇavako verahaccānigottāya brāhmaṇiyā paṭissutvā yenāyasmā udāyī tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ udāyiṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn35.133:2.3: “adhivāsetu kira, bhavaṁ udāyī amhākaṁ ācariyabhariyāya verahaccānigottāya brāhmaṇiyā svātanāya bhattan”ti.
root_text.sn35.133:2.5: Atha kho āyasmā udāyī tassā rattiyā accayena pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya yena verahaccānigottāya brāhmaṇiyā nivesanaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā paññatte āsane nisīdi.
root_text.sn35.133:2.6: Atha kho verahaccānigottā brāhmaṇī āyasmantaṁ udāyiṁ paṇītena khādanīyena bhojanīyena sahatthā santappesi sampavāresi.
root_text.sn35.133:2.7: Atha kho verahaccānigottā brāhmaṇī āyasmantaṁ udāyiṁ bhuttāviṁ onītapattapāṇiṁ pādukā ārohitvā ucce āsane nisīditvā sīsaṁ oguṇṭhitvā āyasmantaṁ udāyiṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn35.133:3.1: Dutiyampi kho so māṇavako yenāyasmā udāyī tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmatā udāyinā saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.sn35.133:3.2: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi.
root_text.sn35.133:3.3: Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho taṁ māṇavakaṁ āyasmā udāyī dhammiyā kathāya sandassesi samādapesi samuttejesi sampahaṁsesi.
root_text.sn35.133:3.4: Dutiyampi kho so māṇavako āyasmatā udāyinā dhammiyā kathāya sandassito samādapito samuttejito sampahaṁsito uṭṭhāyāsanā yena verahaccānigottā brāhmaṇī tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā verahaccānigottaṁ brāhmaṇiṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn35.133:3.6: Samaṇo udāyī dhammaṁ deseti ādikalyāṇaṁ majjhekalyāṇaṁ pariyosānakalyāṇaṁ, sātthaṁ sabyañjanaṁ kevalaparipuṇṇaṁ parisuddhaṁ brahmacariyaṁ pakāsetī”ti.
root_text.sn35.133:4.1: “Evamevaṁ pana tvaṁ, māṇavaka, samaṇassa udāyissa vaṇṇaṁ bhāsasi.
root_text.sn35.133:4.2: Samaṇo panudāyī ‘bhaṇa, samaṇa, dhamman’ti vutto samāno ‘bhavissati, bhagini, samayo’ti vatvā uṭṭhāyāsanā pakkanto”ti.
root_text.sn35.133:4.3: “Tathā hi pana tvaṁ, bhoti, pādukā ārohitvā ucce āsane nisīditvā sīsaṁ oguṇṭhitvā etadavoca:
root_text.sn35.133:4.6: “Tena hi tvaṁ, māṇavaka, mama vacanena samaṇaṁ udāyiṁ nimantehi svātanāya bhattenā”ti.
root_text.sn35.133:4.7: “Evaṁ, bhotī”ti kho so māṇavako verahaccānigottāya brāhmaṇiyā paṭissutvā yenāyasmā udāyī tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ udāyiṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn35.133:4.8: “adhivāsetu kira bhavaṁ udāyī amhākaṁ ācariyabhariyāya verahaccānigottāya brāhmaṇiyā svātanāya bhattan”ti.
root_text.sn35.133:5.1: Atha kho āyasmā udāyī tassā rattiyā accayena pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya yena verahaccānigottāya brāhmaṇiyā nivesanaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā paññatte āsane nisīdi.
root_text.sn35.133:5.2: Atha kho verahaccānigottā brāhmaṇī āyasmantaṁ udāyiṁ paṇītena khādanīyena bhojanīyena sahatthā santappesi sampavāresi.
root_text.sn35.133:5.3: Atha kho verahaccānigottā brāhmaṇī āyasmantaṁ udāyiṁ bhuttāviṁ onītapattapāṇiṁ pādukā orohitvā nīce āsane nisīditvā sīsaṁ vivaritvā āyasmantaṁ udāyiṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn35.133:5.4: “kismiṁ nu kho, bhante, sati arahanto sukhadukkhaṁ paññapenti, kismiṁ asati arahanto sukhadukkhaṁ na paññapentī”ti?
root_text.sn35.133:6.1: “Cakkhusmiṁ kho, bhagini, sati arahanto sukhadukkhaṁ paññapenti, cakkhusmiṁ asati arahanto sukhadukkhaṁ na paññapenti …pe…
root_text.sn35.133:6.2: jivhāya sati arahanto sukhadukkhaṁ paññapenti, jivhāya asati arahanto sukhadukkhaṁ na paññapenti …pe….
root_text.sn35.133:6.3: Manasmiṁ sati arahanto sukhadukkhaṁ paññapenti, manasmiṁ asati arahanto sukhadukkhaṁ na paññapentī”ti.
root_text.sn35.133:7.1: Evaṁ vutte, verahaccānigottā brāhmaṇī āyasmantaṁ udāyiṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn35.133:7.3: Seyyathāpi, bhante, nikkujjitaṁ vā ukkujjeyya, paṭicchannaṁ vā vivareyya, mūḷhassa vā maggaṁ ācikkheyya, andhakāre vā telapajjotaṁ dhāreyya: ‘cakkhumanto rūpāni dakkhantī’ti; evamevaṁ ayyena udāyinā anekapariyāyena dhammo pakāsito.
root_text.sn35.133:7.4: Esāhaṁ, ayya udāyi, taṁ bhagavantaṁ saraṇaṁ gacchāmi, dhammañca, bhikkhusaṅghañca.
root_text.sn35.133:7.5: Upāsikaṁ maṁ ayyo udāyī dhāretu ajjatagge pāṇupetaṁ saraṇaṁ gatan”ti.

translation_text.sn35.133:1.1: At one time Venerable Udāyī was staying near Kāmaṇḍā in the brahmin Todeyya’s mango grove.
translation_text.sn35.133:1.2: Then a boy who was a pupil of the brahmin lady of the Verahaccāni clan went up to Udāyī and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.sn35.133:1.3: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side.
translation_text.sn35.133:1.4: Udāyī educated, encouraged, fired up, and inspired that young student with a Dhamma talk.
translation_text.sn35.133:1.5: Then that young student went to the brahmin lady of the Verahaccāni clan and said to her,
translation_text.sn35.133:1.7: The ascetic Udāyī proclaims a teaching that is good in the beginning, good in the middle, and good in the end, meaningful and well-phrased. And he reveals a spiritual practice that’s entirely full and pure.”
translation_text.sn35.133:2.1: “Then, young student, invite him in my name for tomorrow’s meal.”
translation_text.sn35.133:2.2: “Yes, madam,” he replied. He went to Udāyī and said,
translation_text.sn35.133:2.3: “Sir, would Mister Udāyī please accept an offering of tomorrow’s meal from my tutor’s wife, the brahmin lady of the Verahaccāni clan?”
translation_text.sn35.133:2.5: Then when the night had passed, Udāyī robed up in the morning and, taking his bowl and robe, went to the brahmin lady’s home, and sat down on the seat spread out.
translation_text.sn35.133:2.6: Then the brahmin lady served and satisfied Udāyī with her own hands with delicious fresh and cooked foods.
translation_text.sn35.133:2.7: When Udāyī had eaten and washed his hand and bowl, she put on a pair of slippers, sat on a high seat, covered her head, and said to him,
translation_text.sn35.133:2.9: “There will be an occasion for that, sister,” he replied, then got up from his seat and left.
translation_text.sn35.133:3.1: For a second time that young student went to Venerable Udāyī …
translation_text.sn35.133:3.4: And for a second time that young student went to the brahmin lady of the Verahaccāni clan …
translation_text.sn35.133:4.1: She said to him, “You keep praising the ascetic Udāyī like this.
translation_text.sn35.133:4.2: But when I asked him to preach the Dhamma he just said that there would be an occasion for that, and then he got up and left.”
translation_text.sn35.133:4.3: “Madam, that’s because you put on a pair of slippers, sat on a high seat, and covered your head before inviting him to teach.
translation_text.sn35.133:4.5: For the good fellows respect the teaching.”
translation_text.sn35.133:4.6: “Then, young student, invite him in my name for tomorrow’s meal.”
translation_text.sn35.133:5.2: Then the brahmin lady served and satisfied Udāyī with her own hands with delicious fresh and cooked foods.
translation_text.sn35.133:5.3: When Udāyī had eaten and washed his hand and bowl, she took off her slippers, sat on a low seat, uncovered her head, and said to him,
translation_text.sn35.133:5.4: “Sir, when what exists do the perfected ones declare that there is pleasure and pain? When what doesn’t exist do the perfected ones not declare that there is pleasure and pain?”
translation_text.sn35.133:6.1: “Sister, when there’s an eye, the perfected ones declare that there is pleasure and pain. When there’s no eye, the perfected ones don’t declare that there is pleasure and pain.
translation_text.sn35.133:6.2: When there’s an ear … nose … tongue … body …
translation_text.sn35.133:6.3: mind, the perfected ones declare that there is pleasure and pain. When there’s no mind, the perfected ones don’t declare that there is pleasure and pain.”
translation_text.sn35.133:7.1: When he said this, the brahmin lady said to Udāyī,
translation_text.sn35.133:7.3: As if he were righting the overturned, or revealing the hidden, or pointing out the path to the lost, or lighting a lamp in the dark so people with clear eyes can see what’s there, Master Udāyī has made the teaching clear in many ways.
translation_text.sn35.133:7.4: I go for refuge to the Buddha, to the teaching, and to the mendicant Saṅgha.
translation_text.sn35.133:7.5: From this day forth, may Master Udāyī remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge for life.”

variant_text.sn35.133:1.6: bhoti, jāneyyāsi → bhoti jāneyya (bj, pts1ed, mr); bhotī jāneyya (sya-all, km)

reference_text.sn35.133:1.1: csp1ed13.338, csp2ed13.338, dr18.153, ms13S4_764, msdiv133, pts-vp-pli4.122
